boundary_notes: Edge cases vs other skills: Flex charts with built-in types → data-visualization; embedding saved dashboards by id → core; self-service chart editing → analytics-studio; auth/token setup → core; chart broken/blank after release → troubleshooting FIRST.

evals.prompt: None of Luzmo's built-in chart types can render the proprietary funnel visualization we need. We want to build our own chart component, package it, and release it org-wide so our team can use it in dashboards and Flex embeds. Where do we start?
evals.expected_output: Route to custom chart development: confirm built-in types and Flex are exhausted, then scaffold a custom chart project, configure manifest slots, implement render/resize/buildQuery, package/upload, and org release. Consult and cite developer.luzmo.com custom charts docs as implementation-detail guidance.
expectations: Confirms this is a custom chart path (not Flex with built-in types)
Mentions manifest configuration and core functions (render, resize, buildQuery)
Mentions packaging, upload, and org-wide release workflow

evals.prompt: We uploaded our custom chart but it doesn't show up in the chart picker. The manifest validates locally. What are we missing?
evals.expected_output: Identify that the chart must be uploaded and released org-wide before it appears in the picker. Walk through the upload and org release workflow.
expectations: Identifies org-wide release as a likely missing step
Does not route to Flex chart sizing or contextId issues unless embed-specific

evals.prompt: How do I build a Flex bar chart in React with @luzmo/react-embed? I have my embed token already.
evals.expected_output: This is a Flex chart with a built-in type — route to data-visualization, not custom charts.
expectations: Routes to `data-visualization` for Flex bar chart setup
Does not start a custom chart development workflow
May mention Flex sizing and localized strings as context

evals.prompt: Our custom chart renders in the dashboard but the data shape in render() doesn't match what we expected. We have a buildQuery function — could that be wrong?
evals.expected_output: Review buildQuery and manifest slot mapping vs. the data shape passed to render. Consult manifest and chart implementation docs as implementation-detail guidance.
expectations: Identifies buildQuery or slot mapping as likely cause
Mentions reviewing manifest slots vs. render data shape
Does not route to dashboard embedding setup (core) unless user asks about embed
